label: Network Topologies
description: Refers to the arrangement of different elements like nodes, links, and devices in a network; defines how these components are connected and interact with each other.

label: Types of Network Topologies
description: Categories of network topologies, including point-to-point, bus, ring, star, mesh, tree, and hybrid.

label: Network Topology Applications
description: Used in network designing algorithms, routing algorithms in operating systems, image recognition, data compression, and database management systems.

label: Point to Point Topology
description: A network topology that connects two nodes or devices, where one acts as a sender and the other as a receiver, providing high bandwidth and high speed.

label: Bluetooth Connection
description: An example of Point to Point Topology for file transfer between two devices.

label: Cable Connection (Two Computers)
description: An example of Point to Point Topology for direct connection between two computers.

label: Telephonic Line Connection
description: An example of Point to Point Topology for a telephone line connection between two modems.

label: Satellite Communication Link
description: An example of Point to Point Topology for communication link between two ground stations.

label: Bus Topology
description: A topology where every device is connected to a single coaxial cable called the backbone cable, allowing only one device to send information at a time.

label: CSMA Technique
description: A technique used in Bus Topology to handle data flow and guarantee data integrity.

label: Collision Avoidance (CSMA/CA)
description: A CSMA technique where the sender waits until the transmission media becomes idle if it is busy.

label: Collision Detection (CSMA/CD)
description: A CSMA technique where the sender stops transmitting data if a collision is detected, working on 'recovery after the collision'.

label: Ethernet LAN Cable
description: An example of Bus Topology commonly used in LAN and small offices.

label: Bus Topology Advantages
description: Works excellently in small networks, has low cable and installation cost, supports up to 10 Mbps, and a single node failure does not affect others.

label: Bus Topology Disadvantages
description: Common cable failure crashes the whole system, adding devices slows down the network, low privacy, and high chance of data loss with many connected devices.

label: Ring Topology
description: A topology where all PCs are connected with each other in the form of a ring, and data moves from one node to another either clockwise or anticlockwise.

label: Token Passing
description: A method of transferring data in Ring Topology where the sender modifies a token with the destination address and data, which is passed until it matches the destination.

label: Ring Topology Advantages
description: High-speed data transmission, inexpensive twisted pair cabling with low installation cost, easy device addition/removal, and reduced collision chances due to unidirectional flow.

label: Ring Topology Disadvantages
description: A single fault in the main cable disrupts communication for all nodes, failure of a single node causes the entire network to fail, and communication delay is proportional to the number of nodes.

label: Star Topology
description: A configuration where all nodes are connected to a central hub using a communication link, with the hub functioning as a server.

label: Hub (Star Topology)
description: A central point in Star Topology that functions as a server to control and manage the entire network.

label: Star Topology Advantages
description: Easy network maintenance and management, simple addition of new devices, support for bandwidth up to 100 Mbps, and easy expandability.

label: Star Topology Disadvantages
description: Failure of the central hub brings down the entire network, higher cable requirements, and being more expensive than bus and ring topologies.

label: Mesh Topology
description: A topology where all computers are interconnected with each other through various redundant connections, providing multiple paths for sending and receiving data.

label: Internet
description: One of the best examples of Mesh Topology.

label: Wi-Fi Router
description: An example of Mesh Topology.

label: Smart Home Systems
description: An example of Mesh Topology where multiple smart devices are interconnected.

label: Mesh Topology Advantages
description: Very fast communication speed by choosing the shortest path, non-disruptive addition of new devices, and easier fault diagnosis.

label: Mesh Topology Disadvantages
description: Difficult installation and configuration, and high cost for installation and maintenance.

label: Tree Topology
description: A topology that combines characteristics of bus and star topologies, connecting computers in a hierarchical fashion with point-to-point or point-to-multipoint links.

label: Root Node
description: The main node in a Tree Topology, at the top of the hierarchy.

label: Child Node
description: Nodes connected to a root node or other child nodes in a Tree Topology, forming a hierarchical structure.

label: Tree Topology Advantages
description: Suitability for long-distance data transmission, easy network expansion and management, and very easy error detection and correction due to sub-parts.

label: Tree Topology Disadvantages
description: Failure of the central hub (root node) causes the entire system to fail, and high cost due to extensive cabling.

label: Hybrid Topology
description: A combination of various different topologies, created based on the specific requirements of the network.

label: Star-Ring Topology
description: A commonly used hybrid topology that combines star and ring topologies.

label: Star-Bus Topology
description: A commonly used hybrid topology that combines star and bus topologies.

label: Hybrid Topology Advantages
description: Very flexible, easily expandable by adding new devices, and designable according to network requirements.

label: Hybrid Topology Disadvantages
description: Very difficult to design the architecture and very expensive.

label: Network Topology
description: Arrangement of nodes of a computer network, viewed as a layout of a computer network.

label: Nodes
description: Components that need to be arranged to establish communication in a network.

label: Communication
description: The establishment of data exchange among all nodes in a network.

label: Physical Topology
description: The placement of various nodes in a network.

label: Logical Topology
description: How the data is going to flow in the network.

label: Common Transmission Medium (Bus)
description: A single wire or cable in bus topology capable of sending and receiving data bidirectionally.

label: Advantages of Bus Topology
description: Benefits of bus topology, including less expense, suitability for temporary networks, and node failures not affecting others.

label: Disadvantages of Bus Topology
description: Drawbacks of bus topology, including not being fault tolerant, limited cable length, and no security.

label: Token (Ring Topology)
description: A mechanism in ring topology that dictates which node gets to send data, circulated throughout the network.

label: Advantages of Ring Topology
description: Benefits of ring topology, including better performance than bus topology and all nodes having equal access.

label: Disadvantages of Ring Topology
description: Drawbacks of ring topology, including unidirectional communication, single point of failure affecting the whole network, performance degradation with high load, and no security.

label: Central Node (Hub/Switch)
description: A central device in a star topology that performs centralized administration and through which all network traffic passes.

label: Advantages of Star Topology
description: Benefits of star topology, including ease of design and implementation, centralized administration, and scalability.

label: Disadvantages of Star Topology
description: Drawbacks of star topology, including a single point of failure affecting the whole network, bottlenecks due to overloaded hub/switch, and increased cost.

label: Extended Star Topology
description: A variation of star topology where two or more star topologies are connected.

label: Advantages of Mesh Topology
description: Benefits of mesh topology, including being fault tolerant and reliable.

label: Disadvantages of Mesh Topology
description: Drawbacks of mesh topology, including issues with broadcasting messages, being expensive, and impractical for large networks.
